conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for? If yes, could you please tell me their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. My son Alex is 21 years old. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information about Alex, who is 21 years old.

Since Alex is over 20, I need to ask some important questions about his circumstances:

Is Alex currently enrolled in approved education or training? For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He actually works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask one more important question to complete my assessment:

Do you live in the UK, or are you otherwise subject to UK jurisdiction for benefits purposes (such as being a UK national working abroad for an eligible employer, or covered under certain international agreements)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the situation:

- Alex is 21 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He works full-time
- You live in the UK

Based on the Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit generally stops when a young person reaches 20 years old, or when they turn 20 if they're in approved education or training (it can continue until they finish the course or reach their 20th birthday, whichever comes first, if they started before turning 20).

Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he is over the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Unfortunately, Alex does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit. The benefit is available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved full-time education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, he is beyond the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education; Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is generally available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old and not in qualifying education or training, he does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who is 21 years old, works full-time, and is not in education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit, which ends at age 20 (or when a young person in approved education/training turns 20). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is generally available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old and not in qualifying education or training, he does not meet the age requirements for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who is 21 years old, works full-time, and is not in education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit, which ends at age 20 (or when a young person in approved education/training turns 20). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
